response_text:
This is false. Rust does not have a garbage collector. Instead, Rust uses a system of ownership with rules that the compiler checks at compile time. This system ensures memory safety without needing a garbage collector. 

In Rust, memory is managed through a system of ownership, borrowing, and lifetimes. When a variable goes out of scope, Rust automatically deallocates the memory. This approach provides performance benefits and deterministic resource management, as there is no runtime overhead associated with garbage collection.
